title: Diphasiastrum novoguineense
intro: Diphasiastrum novoguineense, commonly known as the New Guinea fork fern or forked club-moss, is a species of club moss in the family Lycopodiaceae. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits, including its ability to lower blood pressure, improve circulation, and act as an anti-inflammatory agent. The bioactive constituents of Diphasiastrum novoguineense include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic compounds, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of health conditions, from cardiovascular disease to respiratory issues.

This species is further classified in the genus Diphasiastrum, which includes a group of evergreen, mat-forming or shrub-like lycopods. Diphasiastrum novoguineense is a species of lycopod that is native to New Guinea and surrounding islands. It belongs to the division Lycopodiophyta, which is a group of vascular plants that are characterized by their spore-producing reproductive structures. The classification of Diphasiastrum novoguineense reflects its unique characteristics and evolutionary history as a member of the ancient and diverse group of lycopods.
